creator: fstd.lkml@gmail.com
text:
The code reads from standard input, closing that with Ctrl-D produces a visible "^D" (as expected)
The code then goes on to printf(" \t"); -- The blank overwrites the '^', the tab does not blank out the 'D' but merely advances the cursor to the next tabstop.

This is all expected behavior (which can be disabled with `stty -echoctl`)
